chunks.text: THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Crl. Appeal No.S- 53 of 2021 (Muhammad Yaqoob & others v. The State) Date of hearing Order With Signature Of Judge. Hearing of Case 1. For orders on MA 2134/2024 (Permission) 2. For orders on MA 2135/2024 (Compromise) 3. For hearing of main case. M/s Zulfiqar Ali Panhwar and Deedar Ali M. Chohan, Advocates along with appellants. Mr. Nusrat Hussain Memon, Advocate for complainant. Mr. Aftab Ahmed Shar, Additional P.G for the State. Complainant Sajid Ali, injured Muhammad Saleem, Aijaz Ali and Shahid Qadir are present. Date of Hearing: 20-05-2024 Date of Decision: 20-05-2024 J U D G M E N T MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J.,- Appellants Muhammad Yaqoob, Zafar Ali, Ghulam Mustafa and Allah Dino so also complainant Sajid Ali, injured Muhammad Saleem, Aijaz Ali and Shahid Qadir have filed applications supported with affidavits of complainant and injured in terms of sections 345 and 346 CrPC seeking compounding of the offences and their acquittal on the basis of compromise. 2. Today, complainant Sajid Ali, injured Muhammad Saleem, Aijaz Ali and Shahid Qadir have appeared before this Court and admitted their signatures/thumb impressions on their affidavits and contents of the same. They have further stated to have voluntarily forgiven the appellants in the name of Almighty ALLAH and on the intervention of Nekmards of the locality without any coercion, compulsion or inducement and do not claim in lieu of the compromise. They have recorded no objection to the acquittal of appellants. 3. Learned counsel appearing on behalf of complainant and learned Additional P.G have raised no objection if the appellants are acquitted by way of compromise.

They have recorded no objection to the acquittal of appellants. 3. Learned counsel appearing on behalf of complainant and learned Additional P.G have raised no objection if the appellants are acquitted by way of compromise. 2 Crl.Appeal No.S-53 of 2021 4. Therefore, in my humble view and looking to the circumstances of this case and since the offences with which the appellants are charged are compoundable, the compromise arrived at between the parties is found voluntary and genuine. Accordingly, for better relations between the parties in future, the compromise applications are accepted. Appellants Muhammad Yaqoob, Zafar Ali, Ghulam Mustafa and Allah Dino are acquitted of the charge on the basis of compromise. They are present on bail. Their bail bonds stand cancelled and surety(ies) discharged. Office is directed to return surety documents to the surety(ies) after proper verification, identification and as per rules. 5. This Crl. Appeal alongwith pending applications stands disposed of in above terms. JUDGE Ahmad
